title: robata-grilled, aerated portuguese teque os bowl
description: a light, peppery portuguese dish featuring pre-fried teque os and aromatics, served in a kazan cauldron for an ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly meal.
instructions: heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large kazan cauldron over medium heat.
add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
add the teque os and cook until lightly browned, about 2-3 minutes.
add the water, red pepper flakes, salt, chopped cilantro, and lemon juice to the cauldron.
bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the teque os have absorbed most of the liquid.
preheat a robata grill to medium-high heat.
add the teque os mixture to the robata grill and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until lightly charred and puffed.
serve the teque os hot in the kazan cauldron, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
